message: What is the EXACT model and training configuration that produces the publication digital twins of mouse V1 — the Sinz/Tolias 2022 digital-twins reconstruction paper, and the Sensorium 2022/2023 winning models in the sinzlab stack (nnvision, sensorium, neuralpredictors)? Be concrete and cite repo configs and paper methods. Specifically: (1) Is the encoding model exactly the Sensorium-2022 baseline — Stacked2dCore 4 layers, input_kern 9, hidden_kern 7, 64 channels, depth-separable, gamma_input 6.3831, gamma_readout 0.0076, FullGaussian2d readout with a cortex grid_mean_predictor — or a larger or different production model? (2) Do the publication twins INCLUDE behavioral covariates (running speed, pupil size and its derivative) and an eye-position SHIFTER network, which the baseline config can turn off? (3) Is the publication twin a SINGLE model, or an ENSEMBLE of multiple seeds or models averaged together? (4) Training details — optimizer (Adam, lr 0.009), Poisson loss, early stopping on validation correlation, max_iter, the lr-decay schedule — exactly the standard_trainer defaults, or different? (5) What normalized correlation do the published single-model versus ensemble twins reach (the 0.61 figure) — single or ensemble, and averaged over how many stimulus repeats? Context: I trained a SINGLE Sensorium-baseline twin on CPU with behavior OFF and shifter OFF and got median 0.26 raw correlation, and I want to know exactly what differs from how the publication twins are built.
